prompt: A file tailer persists a JSON checkpoint so a new process can resume reading. When the active path is replaced, the restarted reader applies the previous byte offset to unrelated file contents and skips data. Repair checkpoint provenance and seek reconciliation while preserving normal append/resume behavior and existing callers. Edit only candidate source files.

repo_files.file_tailer.py:
from tail_checkpoint import CheckpointStore


class FileTailer:
    def __init__(self, source_path, checkpoint_path):
        self.source_path = source_path
        self.checkpoints = CheckpointStore(checkpoint_path)

    def read_new(self):
        checkpoint = self.checkpoints.load()
        with open(self.source_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as source:
            source.seek(checkpoint.offset)
            lines = [line.rstrip("\n") for line in source]
            self.checkpoints.save(source.tell())
        return lines
repo_files.tests/test_public.py:
from file_tailer import FileTailer


def test_first_read_returns_existing_lines(tmp_path):
    source = tmp_path / "service.log"
    checkpoint = tmp_path / "service.cursor"
    source.write_text("ready\nserving\n", encoding="utf-8")

    assert FileTailer(source, checkpoint).read_new() == ["ready", "serving"]


def test_subsequent_reader_resumes_after_saved_offset(tmp_path):
    source = tmp_path / "service.log"
    checkpoint = tmp_path / "service.cursor"
    source.write_text("first\n", encoding="utf-8")
    assert FileTailer(source, checkpoint).read_new() == ["first"]

    with source.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
        handle.write("second\nthird\n")

    assert FileTailer(source, checkpoint).read_new() == ["second", "third"]
    assert FileTailer(source, checkpoint).read_new() == []
